evals.prompt: This Next.js route is slow because the page waterfalls three fetches and ships too much client JavaScript. Which skill should I use?
evals.expected_output: The response should choose react-best-practices as the default React/Next.js performance surface and mention the relevant performance categories.
assertions: Response chooses `react-best-practices` as the default surface
Response mentions React or Next.js performance symptoms such as waterfalls, bundle size, hydration, or rerender churn
Response does not route the request to `vercel-react-best-practices` as the primary skill

evals.prompt: Audit this heavy client component for hydration mismatch, rerender churn, and weak server/client boundaries.
evals.expected_output: The response should keep the request on the canonical React performance skill and map the problem to one or two optimization lanes.
assertions: Response mentions hydration, rerender, or server/client boundary analysis
Response suggests narrowing to specific performance categories instead of dumping a generic full rule list

evals.prompt: I need Vercel React guidance for a bundle-size regression in my App Router page.
evals.expected_output: The response should still select react-best-practices for general React/Next.js performance work even when Vercel is mentioned.
assertions: Response chooses `react-best-practices` for the general performance request
Response treats Vercel guidance as part of the canonical skill rather than a separate alias requirement
Response mentions bundle size, App Router, or client/server boundaries
